en.extract:
The white-crowned robin-chat (Cossypha albicapillus) is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae.
Its range extends mainly across the western region of the Sudanian savanna.
Its natural habitats are dry savanna and subtropical or tropical moist shrubland.
It has typically been assumed to be monogamous. However, at least one instance of cooperative breeding and possible multiple paternity has been observed.

The '''white-crowned robin-chat''' ('''''Cossypha albicapillus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Muscicapidae]].
Its range extends mainly across the [[West Sudanian savanna|western]] region of the [[Sudanian savanna]].
Its natural [[habitat]]s are dry [[savanna]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[shrubland]].

It has typically been assumed to be monogamous. However, at least one instance of cooperative breeding and possible multiple paternity has been observed.<ref>{{Citation |last=Collar |first=Nigel |title=White-crowned Robin-Chat (''Cossypha albicapillus'') |date=2020-03-04 |work=Birds of the World |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2173/bow.wcrcha1.01 |access-date=2024-04-22 |publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology}}</ref>
[[Image:White-crowned Robin-chat RWD5.jpg|left|thumb|At [[San Diego Zoo]]]]
